brief_document.title: Clean tracked process artifacts from repository
brief_document.purpose: The repo contains ~11 GB of .yggdrasil-worktrees/ plus tracked .meridian/.claude process files, a committed empty server.log, and a .commit-msg.tmp. These are development artifacts that shouldn't be in the repository — they bloat clones, leak process state, and confuse new contributors.
brief_document.task: Remove tracked development artifacts, add appropriate .gitignore entries, and document that worktrees and process files are local-only.

requirements.title: Gitignore coverage
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add .gitignore entries for .yggdrasil-worktrees/, .meridian/, .claude/, server.log, .commit-msg.tmp, and any other development process artifacts.
acceptance: git status shows no untracked process artifacts after the changes.
New worktrees and process files are automatically ignored.

requirements.title: Remove tracked artifacts
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL remove currently tracked development artifacts from the repository index (git rm --cached) without deleting the local files.
acceptance: git ls-files shows no server.log or .commit-msg.tmp entries.
Local files remain on disk — only the git index is cleaned.

requirements.title: Document development setup
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add a CONTRIBUTING.md or development section noting that worktrees and process files are local-only and gitignored.
acceptance: New developers understand that .yggdrasil-worktrees, .meridian, and .claude directories exist locally but aren't committed.
The documentation explains which directories are gitignored and why.
The documentation is discoverable from the root of the repository.

boundaries: SHALL NOT rewrite git history (no git filter-branch or BFG).
SHALL NOT delete local working files — only untrack from git.
SHALL NOT modify .claude/settings.json or any configuration that affects Claude Code behavior.
verification: git status is clean after the changes.
git ls-files shows no process files (.yggdrasil-worktrees, .meridian, .claude, server.log, .commit-msg.tmp).
Local development files remain on disk.
CONTRIBUTING.md or equivalent documentation exists and is accurate.

constraints.text: No modifications to the EventStore trait or conformance suite except where continue_as_new requires a new event variant.
The Recorder's single-writer invariant is never violated.
No backwards compatibility shims — replace, don't add alongside.
No arbitrary limits or hardcoded defaults for configurable values.
The determinism boundary is preserved: workflow.now remains the recorded event timestamp, no wall-clock time in workflow-visible paths.
Content-hash module namespacing invariant is preserved across continue_as_new.

resolved_context.intention: Fix the seam gaps identified in the external code review that prevent the engine from functioning as a production durable workflow system. Each brief addresses a specific gap where components exist in isolation but are not wired together: crash recovery is stubbed, timers are in-memory-only, NIF contracts mismatch the SDK, the WebSocket route is unmounted, and continue_as_new is missing from the SDK.
